titleStmt.title: Letter from Louis C.P. Smith to Sir Malachy Kelly, 15 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Louis C.P. Smith

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Crown Solicitor in Cavan, Louis C.P. Smith (1867-1923) to the Chief Crown Solicitor of Ireland Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916). In the correspondence the two are discussing a case that involved the forgery of a Birth Certificate.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3908

correspAction.placeName: Crown Solicitor's Office, County Cavan, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy.
CROWN SOLICITOR'S OFFICE, CAVAN, 13th day of December 1915
Ulster Winter Assizes, 1915.
County of Cavan.
The King v John Graham. Forgery of Certificate
of Birth Dear Sir, I herewith enclose you by Return
for the Ulster Winter Assizes for the County
of Cavan, which I hope you will find in order. This is the second time which the
Prisoner has been tried and on both occasions
the Jury disagreed. At the trial at the Cavan
Assizes Mr. Justice Gibson took a very serious
view of the case. Judge Kenny on the other
hand although he considered it a serious
matter, seemed more or less disposed to be
satisfied if the Jury brought in a Verdict of
Not Guilty, but said they would have great
difficulty in doing so. The Prisoner stands out
on the same Bail to the next Assizes for this
County. Having regard to his position it is
very hard to get a conviction here, although
the case is perfectly clear. Yours faithfully Louis C P Smith
Sir M Kelly Chief Crown Solr. The Castle Dublin Seen. Submit papers before
next Assizes
J. G.
14' 12' 15. Mr. Smith C S
Please see Attorney
General's Directions
M. Kelly
15-12-15
